growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.strain: bioreactor isolate (Node 55; 99.99% ANI to metagenome MAG; closest GTDB relative Variovorax sp003019815, GCF_003019815.1)

sec.method: reported max extracellular thiamine titre in 5 mg/L pantothenate-supplemented culture (produced linearly during lag phase, then consumed at onset of exponential growth)

growth_records.cite: Hessler et al. Vitamin interdependencies predicted by metagenomics-informed network analyses and validated in microbial community microcosms. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-40360-4. [PMC10409787]
growth_records.snippet: Higher concentrations of supplied pantothenate corresponded with greater produced thiamine concentrations, with a maximum of 39.5 mg/L thiamine observed in the 5 mg/L pantothenate supplemented cultured.
growth_records.notes: Variovorax (pantothenate auxotroph) grown in liquid M9 minimal media (0.4 g/L glucose base) with 5 mg/L pantothenate. Max extracellular thiamine titre 39.5 mg/L (highest across the pantothenate titration). Thiamine produced linearly during an extended lag phase, then rapidly re-consumed at the onset of exponential growth. No growth rate (μ) or biomass-specific rate reported numerically (OD time-courses only in Fig 7a; text notes specific thiamine production per unit OD was roughly constant across pantothenate levels but gives no number), so growth_rate/doubling/qS left null. Temperature 30 °C (isolate culturing incubation). Second NaCl entry is transcribed verbatim from Methods (likely a typo for a second salt); mM concentrations of phosphate salts as written are unusually dilute but recorded as stated. No specific volumetric thiamine rate computed because time-to-max at this condition is not stated.

growth_records.strain: bioreactor isolate (Node 55; closest GTDB relative Variovorax sp003019815)

sec.units: mg/L over 144 h (extracellular titre); ~0.058 mg/L/h volumetric (calculated)
sec.method: reported extracellular thiamine titre 8.4 mg/L over first 144 h with no supplied pantothenate; volumetric rate = 8.4 mg/L / 144 h = 0.058 mg/L/h

growth_records.cite: Hessler et al. Vitamin interdependencies predicted by metagenomics-informed network analyses and validated in microbial community microcosms. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-40360-4. [PMC10409787]
growth_records.snippet: Variovorax showed very little growth within the first 144 h in the absence of supplied pantothenate, yet still produced considerable quantities of extracellular thiamine (8.4 mg/L) over the first 144 h.
growth_records.notes: Variovorax in M9 minimal media WITHOUT supplemented pantothenate: very little growth in first 144 h, yet 8.4 mg/L extracellular thiamine accumulated over that window. Volumetric thiamine production rate = 8.4 mg/L ÷ 144 h = 0.058 mg/L/h (arithmetic from the two explicit numbers; NOT biomass-specific because biomass/OD over this window is not given as a number). No μ or specific rate reported (little growth), so growth_rate/doubling/qS null. Temperature 30 °C.

growth_records.strain: bioreactor isolate (Node 55; closest GTDB relative Variovorax sp003019815)

sec.method: reported extracellular thiamine rose from 3.6 mg/L (start) to a maximum of 100 mg/L after 96 h; volumetric rate = (100 - 3.6) mg/L / 96 h = 1.00 mg/L/h

growth_records.cite: Hessler et al. Vitamin interdependencies predicted by metagenomics-informed network analyses and validated in microbial community microcosms. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-40360-4. [PMC10409787]
growth_records.snippet: The spent media enabled Variovorax growth from 3.6 mg/L at the start of the experiment to a maximum of 100 mg/L after 96 h. ... optical density (OD) of ~0.15 after 96 h (Fig. 6c), but in an almost linear fashion.
growth_records.notes: Variovorax pure culture in M9 minimal media + 25% Cryptococcus spent media (pantothenate source; Cryptococcus spent media measured ~5 mg/L pantothenate). Extracellular thiamine rose 3.6 -> 100 mg/L over 96 h (headline 'up to 100 mg/L'). Volumetric thiamine production rate = (100 - 3.6) mg/L ÷ 96 h = 1.00 mg/L/h (arithmetic from explicit numbers). Culture reached OD600 ~0.15 after 96 h in an almost linear fashion; growth was near-linear (not exponential) so a specific μ is not meaningful/derivable and biomass-specific qP cannot be computed (only start OD ~0.035 from co-culture Methods and end OD ~0.15 given), so growth_rate/doubling left null. Temperature 30 °C. Related datum (not a rate): in Variovorax+Cryptococcus co-culture Variovorax rose from 7x10^5 to 9x10^8 cells/ml (~130-fold) vs pure culture.
